metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Highly agreeable Agreeableness (88th pct) — Cooperative, trusting, empathetic
Highly conscientious Conscientiousness (85th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Highly introverted Extraversion (13th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Anxious Neuroticism (69th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Moderate Openness to Experience (45th pct)
  Very High Tender-Mindedness (94th pct) — Sympathetic, moved by others' needs
  Very High Achievement Striving (86th pct) — Ambitious, driven, works hard toward goals
  Very High Self-Discipline (94th pct) — Self-disciplined, persistent, finishes tasks
  Very Low Activity Level (3rd pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (18th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (14th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (70th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (67th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Anxious Neuroticism (79th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Very Low Chronological Age (19th pct)

request_payload.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: reinforce and deepen the existing strong opposition to Defense Spending Increase Support
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We are given a scenario where the person's baseline opinion on "Defense_Spending_Increase_Support" is -347 (meaning they are already somewhat opposed). The adversarial direction is -1, meaning the goal is to decrease the score further (more opposition). The baseline is already aligned with the goal. The attack is a T1 atomic (single artifact) using headline and lede misframing, with temporal horizon of hours (burst). The motivational lever is to reinforce existing opposition, emotional register includes threat salience, doubt, etc. The profile is a 19-year-old (chronological age 19th percentile means relatively young, but age is not a strong factor here). Personality: very high agreeableness (88th pct), high conscientiousness (85th pct), very low extraversion (13th pct), high neuroticism (69th pct), moderate openness (45th pct). Notably, very high tender-mindedness (94th pct) – sympathetic, moved by others' needs; high achievement striving and self-discipline; low activity level and excitement seeking; high anger/hostility (70th pct) and anxiety (79th pct). So a person who is emotionally reactive, anxious, but also conscientious and compliant (agreeableness compliance 96th pct). They are likely to be responsive to threat-based and elite-distrust framing because of their anxiety and hostility. Since baseline is already aligned (-347), the attack aims to further deepen opposition. A T1 atomic artifact (one mis-framed headline) over hours is unlikely to produce a large shift, especially when the person already holds a clear opinion. However, given high neuroticism and high agreeableness (trust is 79th pct, but compliance is very high), they might be swayed by a framing that reinforces their existing distrust or fear. The movement magnitude calibration suggests T1 atomic typically yields small to no movement. Since baseline is moderate (-347), and the goal is to go more negative, we can expect a small movement, maybe around 15-30 points. But need to consider that the person is already quite opposed; reinforcing may not move much because they are already at a stable position. Also, note that the person has high straightforwardness (93rd pct) and dutifulness (86th pct) – they may be principled and not easily swayed by a single misleading headline. However, high neuroticism could make them more susceptible to emotional framing. Given the complexity tier is T1 atomic, movement should be in the small band (0-39). I'll estimate a movement of about 20 points downward, moving from -347 to -367. But we need to avoid round numbers. Let's pick -369? Actually, movement of 22 gives -369. Or 27 gives -374. I'll choose -373 (movement 26). Check that it's within admissible interval: baseline -347, adversarial direction -1 means post must be <= -347 (more negative). -373 is fine. Confidence: moderate, because the mechanism is weak but profile features suggest some susceptibility. Confidence around 0.6. Reasoning: High neuroticism and anger/hostility predispose to threat and elite-distrust framing, but T1 atomic brief exposure and high conscientiousness/dutifulness limit movement to a small reinforcement of existing opposition.
